title: Skopeo Sync - Error when copying to destination registry
body:
Hi, 

I'm trying to use Skopeo to sync images from DockerHub to an air-gapped test environment.

My host is an `Ubuntu 21.04 (GNU/Linux 5.11.0-41-generic x86_64)` Skopeo version `skopeo version 1.2.1`. I installed skopeo using `apt`

This is the command that I'm using to copy the images from DockerHub to my local file system:

`skopeo sync --debug --src docker --dest dir docker.io/httpd:2 ../skopeoSync/`

The sync from DockerHub to my local dir seems to complete without issue. 

I then copy the contents of `skopeoSync/` directory to our test environment

This is then the command that I'm using to sync the image from my local dir to the air-gapped registry:

`skopeo sync --debug --src dir --dest docker ../skopeoSync/httpd\:2/ harbor/library`

This is the debug output. When syncing the images from the local dir to the air-gapped registry something is causing `skopeo` to attempt to connect to DockerHub. The test environment is air-gapped it is unable to connect to DockerHub and the sync then fails. I dont understand by the sync from my local dir to the private registry requires access to DockerHub?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

> ```
> INFO[0000] Copying image ref 1/1                         from="dir:../skopeoSync/httpd:2" to="docker://harbor/library/httpd:2"
> ```

Throughout the container ecosystem, in container names  `first/second…`, `first` is only recognized as a host name if one of:
- it is exactly `localhost`,or
- it contains a `.` (i.e. it is a multi-component name like `harbor.example.com`)
- it contains a `:` (i.e. a port specification).

Otherwise it is interpreted either as a reference to `docker.io` (the case here), an alias, or a short name to be found in one of the `unqualified-search-registries`.

So it’s not just `sync`, you’ll need to determine how to refer to the internal registry in other uses like `podman pull` (and that might affect contents of some configuration files, e.g. `registries.conf`, equally). If possible, I’d recommend using an internal domain (`harbor.internal.example.com`) or the like.

In the worst case, it’s possible to use `harbor:443` to conform to the rules (or maybe `harbor:80` if it is not using TLS) without changing the DNS name — but note that most configuration will then have to also always explicitly use `harbor:443`.
@trident25 Does `skopeo sync` work when using a recognized host name syntax, per the above?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing, assuming the above made a difference. Feel free to reopen or speak up if that’s not the case.
